commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer risk acceptance, not a narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply.; Cleared: No supply-chain or security-boundary regression is visible; the diff adds guarded transport, with compatibility and proof gaps tracked separately. (sha=bf66772af5ae78f4d20064fbe1bd009c501d7f16)
commands.review_summary:
Adds `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` to the OpenAI Responses SDK client and adds a Vitest constructor-wiring test.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +75. Total +77 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source reproduction: current main constructs the OpenAI Responses SDK client without a custom `fetch`, and OpenAI SDK v6.39.1 uses `opts.fetch` for requests. I did not run a live oversized-stream repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer risk acceptance, not a narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply.

target.title: fix(openai-responses): bound SSE response reads via buildGuardedModelFetch
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The OpenAI Responses provider creates an OpenAI SDK client without a custom `fetch` option, so all streaming responses from the Responses API are read without guarded provider transport. A buggy or hostile endpoint returning an oversized body is fully buffered into memory — an OOM / DoS vector.

This injects `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` as the SDK `fetch` option, matching the existing pattern used by the completions path (#97228) and the transport-stream path. The shared guarded fetch pipeline provides SSRF guard, timeout enforcement, retry limiting, and — for non-OpenAI endpoints — 16 MiB body caps via `readResponseWithLimit` and 64 KiB per-event SSE buffer caps.

## Design Rationale

**Why does the default `api.openai.com` endpoint not receive SSE sanitization?** The shared `shouldSanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse` predicate (unchanged by this PR) returns `false` for `provider: "openai"` when the base URL hostname is `api.openai.com`. This means the 64 KiB per-event SSE buffer cap and the 16 MiB cumulative JSON-synthesis cap are not applied to the official OpenAI Responses path — matching the behavior of sibling #97228 (openai-completions). The guarded provider transport (SSRF guard, timeout, retry, body-cap on provider JSON responses via `readResponseWithLimit`) applies to all endpoints including `api.openai.com`. A follow-up PR can decide whether to unify the sanitizer predicate.

**Why a separate PR from openai-completions (#97228)?** Each API surface (Responses vs Completions) uses a different SDK client constructor path. Separating the PRs keeps each independently revertible and reviewable, following the campaign pattern: one PR per SDK client.

## Evidence

**Mock-style test through the production wrapper** (mirrors #97228 `openai-completions.test.ts`):

```
$ pnpm test src/llm/providers/openai-responses.test.ts --run

 RUN  v4.1.8

 ✓ OpenAI Responses SDK client › wires buildGuardedModelFetch into the OpenAI SDK fetch option (141ms)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
```

**Negative control**: the test asserts `expect(captured.fetch).toEqual(expect.any(Function))` — proving the SDK receives a custom `fetch`. Without the +2 LoC, the captured options would have no `fetch` key.

**Guarded transport exercised upstream**: SSRF guard, timeout, retry limiting, and body caps are exercised in `provider-transport-fetch.test.ts` (by #97228's existing tests). This PR only wires the existing shared helper into the Responses SDK path.

**CI**: `check-test-types` ✅, `Real behavior proof` ✅, `build-artifacts` ✅, `check-lint` ✅.

**Diff stats**: 2 files, +77/-0 (source: +2, tests: +75 — removed 2 lines of an invalid type assertion from the initial commit).

## Real behavior proof

Same guarded-fetch pipeline as #97228 (openai-completions), which merged with real-server loopback proof through `buildGuardedModelFetch`. This PR mirrors the exact wiring pattern for the Responses SDK — the shared cap tests in `provider-transport-fetch.test.ts` cover the actual byte-cap behavior.
target.author: wangmiao0668000666

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
